title: CPE C2 Proficiency Reading & Use of English - Practice Test 5 (Book 3)
source: CPE Practice Tests 3, Virginia Evans, Express Publishing

sections.title: Part 1: Multiple-Choice Cloze
sections.instructions: For questions 1-8, read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.
sections.passageHtml:
Part 1: Cloze Texts

Noise in Cities

In big cities, people are estimated to endure more than 75 decibels (dB) of noise during the (1) ______ of the day from traffic, night-clubs and building sites. To compare these noise levels to that of a passing train, which creates 70-80 dB. A 1995 EU report states that almost a fifth of Europeans are (2) ______ to high noise levels. Although most complaints are voiced by older people, the young are not (3) ______ to the dangers noise causes. Blood pressure levels of children (4) ______ school in noisy areas were found to be higher than those of children living elsewhere. Loud noise (80-90 dB) for six hours a day begins to affect our hearing and even if we escape deafness in the long term, messages to the brain are (5) ______ and we find that our memory has started to go. Fortunately, the symptoms of stress (6) ______ by noise are preventable.

Green Retailing

Selling 'green' products can be a (7) ______ business amongst Britain's retailers. In the past, the emphasis was on (8) ______ foods of artificial additives, but now the key-word is the 'environment' and retailers are all out to capture this rapidly expanding market.

questionGroups.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.

questions.explanation: Đáp án C. Cấu trúc cố định (collocation) 'during the course of the day' nghĩa là 'trong tiến trình/suốt cả ngày'. Bẫy: 'duration' là thời gian kéo dài của một sự việc cụ thể, 'period' là thời kỳ, 'length' là độ dài vật lý hoặc thời gian. Chuyên đề: Collocations.
questions.answerLocation: during the course of the day
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.explanation: Đáp án D. Cấu trúc bị động 'be exposed to something' nghĩa là 'phơi nhiễm / tiếp xúc với cái gì (thường là tác nhân gây hại)'. Bẫy: 'revealed' là để lộ ra, 'disclosed' là tiết lộ thông tin bảo mật, 'introduced' là giới thiệu. Chuyên đề: Prepositional verbs.
questions.answerLocation: Europeans are exposed to high noise levels.
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.explanation: Đáp án C. Tính từ 'immune' đi kèm giới từ 'to' chỉ khả năng đề kháng, miễn nhiễm: 'not immune to the dangers' (không hề miễn nhiễm trước các hiểm họa). Bẫy: 'exempt from' là được miễn (thuế/nghĩa vụ), 'excluded from' là bị loại trừ khỏi, 'indifferent to' là thờ ơ/dửng dưng với. Chuyên đề: Prepositions after adjectives.
questions.answerLocation: young are not immune to the dangers noise causes.
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.explanation: Đáp án A. Động từ 'attending' dùng trong ngữ cảnh đi học, học tập tại trường: 'children attending school' (trẻ em đang đi học tại trường). Bẫy: 'intending' là dự định, 'participating in' đòi hỏi phải có giới từ 'in', 'frequenting' nghĩa là hay lui tới một nơi chốn giải trí nhưng không dùng cho trường học. Chuyên đề: Vocabulary — Verbs.
questions.answerLocation: children attending school in noisy areas
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.explanation: Đáp án C. Động từ 'disrupted' nghĩa là bị gián đoạn, phá vỡ cấu trúc hoạt động bình thường, phù hợp với sự truyền tín hiệu thông tin lên não: 'messages to the brain are disrupted' (các tín hiệu đến não bị gián đoạn). Bẫy: 'protracted' là bị kéo dài lê thê, 'erupted' là phun trào (núi lửa) hoặc bùng phát (bạo lực), 'distracted' là bị mất tập trung (chỉ con người). Chuyên đề: Vocabulary — Verbs.
questions.answerLocation: messages to the brain are disrupted
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.explanation: Đáp án A. Động từ phân từ 'induced' nghĩa là được gây ra bởi, sinh ra bởi: 'symptoms of stress induced by noise' (các triệu chứng căng thẳng gây ra bởi tiếng ồn). Bẫy: 'reduced' là bị giảm thiểu, 'conducted' là được tiến hành (nghiên cứu), 'inducted' là được bổ nhiệm/kết nạp. Chuyên đề: Vocabulary — Participles. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: symptoms of stress induced by noise are preventable.
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.explanation: Đáp án C. Tính từ 'lucrative' nghĩa là sinh lợi, đem lại nhiều lợi nhuận, thường đi với danh từ 'business' hoặc 'market': 'a lucrative business' (một ngành kinh doanh béo bở/sinh lời). Bẫy: 'prosperous' là thịnh vượng (dùng cho nền kinh tế/quốc gia/con người), 'wealthy' là giàu có (chỉ người), 'commercial' thuộc về thương mại nói chung chứ không mang sắc thái sinh lợi nhuận cao. Chuyên đề: Vocabulary — Adjectives.
questions.answerLocation: green' products can be a lucrative business
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.explanation: Đáp án D. Cấu trúc 'ridding something of something' nghĩa là 'loại bỏ/giải phóng cái gì khỏi cái gì' (emphasis was on ridding foods of artificial additives - nhấn mạnh vào việc loại bỏ các chất phụ gia nhân tạo khỏi thực phẩm). Bẫy: 'depriving someone of something' là tước đoạt của ai cái gì, 'removing' đi với giới từ 'from', 'separating' đi với 'from'. Chuyên đề: Prepositional verbs.
questions.answerLocation: ridding foods of artificial additives
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

sections.title: Part 2: Open Cloze
sections.instructions: For questions 9-16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap.
sections.passageHtml:
Evening Classes

In the 1960s, going to an evening class was something that poor people might do for a night out; it was a leisure thing, something (9) ______ be enjoyed. You tried your hand at anything that took your fancy, (10) ______ with a view to improving your career prospects but simply (11) ______ you wanted to see (12) ______ it was like.

The central theme guiding further education was strictly non-vocational; there was never a particular goal (13) ______ the end of it — it was all done simply (14) ______ fun. A woman once wandered (15) ______ my poetry class by mistake looking for the pottery class and stayed on to (16) ______ a pretty good poet.

questionGroups.instruction: Write the correct word in each gap.

questions.explanation: Đáp án: TO. Cấu trúc to-infinitive bổ nghĩa cho đại từ bất định 'something to be enjoyed' (một thứ để thưởng thức). Chuyên đề: Verb patterns.
questions.answerLocation: something to be enjoyed
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.explanation: Đáp án: NOT. Kết hợp cấu trúc phủ định song hành 'not... but simply because...' (không phải với mục đích... mà chỉ đơn giản là vì...). Chuyên đề: Conjunctions.
questions.answerLocation: not with a view to improving
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.explanation: Đáp án: BECAUSE (hoặc SINCE / AS). Liên từ chỉ nguyên nhân đứng sau trạng từ 'simply': 'but simply because you wanted...' (mà chỉ đơn giản là vì bạn muốn...). Chuyên đề: Conjunctions. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: simply because you wanted to see
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (12): wanted to see ______ it was like.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: WHAT. Cấu trúc nghi vấn gián tiếp 'what it is like' (nó như thế nào) đứng làm tân ngữ sau động từ 'see'. Chuyên đề: Pronouns.
questions.answerLocation: wanted to see what it was like
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (13): particular goal ______ the end of it
questions.explanation: Đáp án: AT (hoặc BY). Cụm giới từ chỉ thời gian/vị trí cuối cùng của một chuỗi sự kiện: 'at the end of it' (ở thời điểm kết thúc khóa học). Chuyên đề: Prepositions.
questions.answerLocation: never a particular goal at the end of it
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.explanation: Đáp án: FOR. Cụm từ cố định 'for fun' (để giải trí / cho vui). Chuyên đề: Fixed expressions. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: all done simply for fun
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.explanation: Đáp án: INTO. Động từ chuyển động 'wander into' nghĩa là đi lang thang / đi lạc vào một không gian khép kín nào đó: 'wandered into my poetry class' (đi lạc vào lớp học thơ của tôi). Chuyên đề: Prepositional verbs.
questions.answerLocation: wandered into my poetry class by mistake
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (16): stayed on to ______ a pretty good poet.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: BECOME. Cấu trúc to-infinitive chỉ kết quả bất ngờ: 'stayed on to become...' (ở lại và cuối cùng trở thành một nhà thơ khá giỏi). Chuyên đề: Verb patterns.
questions.answerLocation: stayed on to become a pretty good poet.
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

sections.title: Part 3: Word Formation
sections.instructions: For questions 17-24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the space in the same line.
sections.passageHtml:
Broadband

Broadband is potentially a real revolution simply because of the (17) ______ (BELIEVE) fast rates at which information can be transferred from one computer to another. Gone will be the days of (18) ______ (END) waiting for that video clip to (19) ______ (LOAD) to your computer — it will all happen in less time than it takes to turn your computer on.

Once you have the (20) ______ (CONNECT), it's fast, very fast. Why then, have people in Britain been so (21) ______ (APPEAR) reluctant to switch to the new service, lagging behind much of western Europe in this respect?

One problem has been the (22) ______ (DIFFICULT) people have encountered actually getting hooked up; it can take months for telephone lines to be (23) ______ (GRADE) so that broadband can be installed, and in some (24) ______ (LOCATE) the service is simply unavailable.

questionGroups.instruction: Write the correct transformed word in each gap.

questions.explanation: Đáp án: UNBELIEVABLY. Chuyển đổi từ gốc động từ 'BELIEVE' thành tính từ 'believable', thêm tiền tố phủ định thành 'unbelievable', rồi chuyển thành trạng từ 'unbelievably' để bổ nghĩa cho tính từ 'fast' đứng sau nó: 'unbelievably fast rates' (tốc độ nhanh đến mức không thể tin nổi). Chuyên đề: Word formation — Adverbs.
questions.answerLocation: unbelievably fast rates at which
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.explanation: Đáp án: ENDLESS (hoặc ENDLESSLY). Danh từ 'END' chuyển thành tính từ 'endless' (vô tận / không bao giờ dứt) bổ nghĩa trực tiếp cho danh từ động từ 'waiting': 'endless waiting' (sự chờ đợi vô tận). Trạng từ 'endlessly' cũng được chấp nhận nếu bổ nghĩa cho hành động chờ đợi. Chuyên đề: Word formation — Adjectives.
questions.answerLocation: days of endless waiting for
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.explanation: Đáp án: DOWNLOAD. Động từ gốc 'LOAD' được thêm tiền tố 'down-' để chỉ hành động tải dữ liệu từ máy chủ mạng về máy tính cá nhân: 'video clip to download to your computer'. Đứng sau cấu trúc 'for something to do'. Chuyên đề: Word formation — Verbs.
questions.answerLocation: that video clip to download to your
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.explanation: Đáp án: CONNECTION. Động từ 'CONNECT' chuyển thành danh từ 'connection' chỉ đường truyền / sự kết nối internet đứng sau mạo từ xác định 'the': 'Once you have the connection'. Chuyên đề: Word formation — Nouns.
questions.answerLocation: Once you have the connection, it's fast
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.explanation: Đáp án: APPARENTLY. Động từ 'APPEAR' chuyển thành tính từ 'apparent', sau đó thêm hậu tố '-ly' thành trạng từ 'apparently' (nhìn có vẻ / hình như là) bổ nghĩa cho cả cụm tính từ 'reluctant to switch': 'so apparently reluctant'. Chuyên đề: Word formation — Adverbs.
questions.answerLocation: been so apparently reluctant to switch
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.explanation: Đáp án: DIFFICULTIES (hoặc DIFFICULTY). Tính từ 'DIFFICULT' chuyển thành danh từ 'difficulty'. Vì đứng sau cấu trúc chỉ nhiều trở ngại do người dùng gặp phải, ta dùng dạng số nhiều 'difficulties' (những khó khăn): 'the difficulties people have encountered'. Chuyên đề: Word formation — Nouns.
questions.answerLocation: the difficulties people have encountered
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.explanation: Đáp án: UPGRADED. Động từ 'GRADE' được thêm tiền tố 'up-' thành 'upgrade' (nâng cấp). Ở đây dùng trong cấu trúc bị động 'to be upgraded' chỉ việc nâng cấp đường dây điện thoại phù hợp lắp đặt broadband. Chuyên đề: Word formation — Verbs.
questions.answerLocation: for telephone lines to be upgraded so that
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.explanation: Đáp án: LOCATIONS. Động từ 'LOCATE' chuyển thành danh từ 'location' (địa điểm, vị trí). Đứng sau từ hạn định số lượng 'some', danh từ phải ở dạng số nhiều: 'in some locations'. Chuyên đề: Word formation — Nouns.
questions.answerLocation: and in some locations the service is
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

sections.title: Part 4: Key Word Transformation
sections.instructions: For questions 25-30, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and eight words, including the word given.
sections.passageHtml:
Part 4: Key Word Transformation

Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the key word in bold. Do not change the word given.

questionGroups.instruction: Complete the second sentence using the key word in bold.

questions.instruction: I was expecting him to be offered the job. (came) -> It ____________________ that he was offered the job.
acceptedAnswers: came as no surprise to me
questions.correctAnswer: came as no surprise to me
questions.explanation: Đáp án: CAME AS NO SURPRISE TO ME (hoặc CAME AS NO SURPRISE WHEN). Sử dụng thành ngữ cố định 'come as no surprise to someone' nghĩa là 'không nằm ngoài dự đoán / không có gì bất ngờ đối với ai'. Đi kèm mệnh đề danh ngữ 'that...'. Chuyên đề: Idioms & Fixed phrases.
questions.answerLocation: came as no surprise to me
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 4, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Thanks to Mary’s help, we were able to rescue them. (not) -> But ____________________ able to rescue them.
acceptedAnswers: for Mary's help we would not have been
for Mary's help we would not be
for Mary's help we could not have been
questions.correctAnswer: for Mary's help we would not have been
questions.explanation: Đáp án: FOR MARY'S HELP WE WOULD NOT HAVE BEEN. Sử dụng cấu trúc điều kiện giả định phủ định 'But for + Noun Phrase' tương đương với 'If it had not been for'. Vế sau phải dùng mệnh đề giả định quá khứ hoàn thành (Conditional Type 3): 'we would not have been able to'. Chuyên đề: Conditionals.
questions.answerLocation: for Mary's help we would not have been
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 4, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: I suddenly realised that he had no idea what I was talking about. (dawned) -> It ____________________ that he had no idea what I was talking about.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: SUDDENLY DAWNED ON ME. Sử dụng cấu trúc giả định chủ ngữ giả 'It dawns on someone that...' nghĩa là 'ai đó chợt nhận ra rằng...'. Trạng từ 'suddenly' được thêm vào để nhấn mạnh tính tức thời. Chuyên đề: Fixed expressions.
questions.answerLocation: suddenly dawned on me
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 4, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Road works delayed us on the way to the airport. (held) -> We ____________________ by road works on the way to the airport.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: WERE HELD UP. Sử dụng phrasal verb 'hold up' ở thể bị động 'be held up' nghĩa là 'bị làm cho chậm trễ / bị trì hoãn'. Chuyên đề: Phrasal verbs & Passives. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: were held up
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 4, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: She found it hard to get used to the idea that I had been made redundant. (terms) -> She found it hard ____________________ I had been made redundant.
acceptedAnswers: to come to terms with the fact that
to come to terms with the idea that
to come to terms with being
to come to terms with having been
questions.correctAnswer: to come to terms with the fact that
questions.explanation: Đáp án: TO COME TO TERMS WITH THE FACT THAT (hoặc TO COME TO TERMS WITH BEING / HAVING BEEN). Sử dụng thành ngữ 'come to terms with something/doing something' nghĩa là 'học cách chấp nhận hoàn cảnh khó khăn/đau khổ'. Đứng sau tính từ cấu trúc 'find it hard + to-infinitive'. Chuyên đề: Idioms.
questions.answerLocation: to come to terms with
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 4, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: They bought the bigger piece of land because they intended to grow their own vegetables. (view) -> They bought the bigger piece of land ____________________ their own vegetables.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: WITH A VIEW TO GROWING. Sử dụng cụm cấu trúc chỉ mục đích cố định 'with a view to + V-ing' nghĩa là 'với ý định/mục tiêu làm gì'. Động từ 'grow' chuyển thành dạng danh động từ 'growing' sau giới từ 'to'. Chuyên đề: Fixed expressions & Gerunds.
questions.answerLocation: with a view to growing
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 4, CPE Practice Tests 3

sections.title: Part 5: Multiple Choice
sections.instructions: You are going to read a review of a book on Victorian life. For questions 31-36, choose the answer (A, B, C or D) which you think fits best according to the text.
sections.passageHtml:
The Victorian Household

In Judith Flanders’ new book, published by HarperCollins, ‘The Victorian House: Domestic Life from Childbirth to Deathbed’, we gain a tantalising insight into the life women led in Victorian times. We do not see them solely in their roles as mothers or wives but we trace them through their lifecycle by following them around their houses, long terraced stretches that increasingly defined what it meant to be genteel and female as the 19th century progressed.

Mapping female biology on to bricks and mortar is a fine conceit and provides an excellent way of ordering material that might otherwise have become unwieldy. As in her previous book, ‘A Circle of Sisters’, Flanders shows herself adept at controlling the flow of information and keeping her story moving briskly forward (there is no dawdling in this house, no glancing backwards or racing ahead). Also impressive is her terrier-like grasp of physical detail: it is easy enough to write blithely about how tenement dwellers would have to carry 22 gallons of water up the stairs every day if they wanted to meet contemporary definitions of being ‘fairly clean’, quite another to work out, as Flanders has, that this is the equivalent of carrying five and a half cases of still water back from the supermarket.

Living in Victorian Britain, and particularly in Victorian London which, although she does not say so, is Flanders’s focus, meant a constant struggle with the dark forces of dirt. No sooner had you soaked, pounded and wrung the household's laundry into a state of approximate cleanliness than a veil of smut and soot would descend on the still-damp linen (it could take up to three days to dry, by which time wash day was looming all over again). Kitchen floors, if left unattended for more than a couple of days, soon became a heaving carpet of beetles and cockroaches. Bed linen was usually infested with fleas, and the writer Beatrix Potter felt sickened at quite how many had found their way into her Torquay hotel room. "It is possible," she recorded gloomily in her diary, "to have too much Natural History in a bed."

As this summary suggests, Flanders has a good eye for the telling anecdote. The only problem is that in a very substantial number of cases it is not she who has found it. ‘The Victorian House’ is heavily dependent on secondary sources, which means that all too often Flanders is quoting someone (often an academic historian, sometimes an anthologist) who is referring to the original source, be it Beatrix Potter’s Journal, ‘The Architect’ magazine, or Charles Dickens’ journalism.

This would be disappointing but not flawed were it not for the fact that in her introduction Flanders describes her modus operandi as conducting a search of “reportage, diaries, letters and journals" in order to build a picture of Victorian life. But by relying on material that has already been sifted and filtered by a generation of historians, the danger is that Flanders is merely reproducing a paradigm of Victorian domestic life that was formed as far back as the mid 1970s, when scholars on both sides of the Atlantic started to look closely at the lives of ordinary women in the 19th century.

There is much that is useful and good about The Victorian House. The illustrations are excellent, especially the drop-in black and white prints, drawn from under-used vernacular sources, such as trade catalogues and advertisements. The problem, really, is Flanders’s voice, which implies an authority that simply is not backed up by her text. In this she is rather like those Victorian manual writers whom she quotes, like Mrs. Beeton, who advised women how to stage a particular kind of gentility which, by the very act of writing so feverishly for the marketplace, they had conspicuously failed to manage for themselves.

questionGroups.instruction: For questions 31-36, choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.

questions.instruction: 31. What does Judith Flanders set out to do in her new book?
options: A. focus on the ingenuity of the Victorians in house decoration
B. give the reader an insight into Victorian life through their homes
C. follow the lifecycle of Victorian families
D. describe the typical architecture and interior design of a Victorian house
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. Ở đoạn 1, tác giả viết: "we gain a tantalising insight into the life women led in Victorian times... by following them around their houses". Điều này cho thấy mục đích chính của cuốn sách là mang lại cho độc giả cái nhìn sâu sắc về cuộc sống của phụ nữ thời đại này thông qua không gian ngôi nhà của họ. Bẫy: (A) sai vì trang trí nhà cửa không phải trọng tâm chính, (C) sai vì cuốn sách tập trung vào phụ nữ chứ không phải cả gia đình, (D) sai vì kiến trúc chỉ là cái khung để dẫn dắt câu chuyện sinh học và đời sống chứ không phải mục đích mô tả kỹ thuật kiến trúc. Chuyên đề: Reading — Detailed understanding.
questions.answerLocation: we gain a tantalising insight into the life women led in Victorian times... by following them around their houses
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 5, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 32. In the second paragraph, what comment does the reviewer make about the writer's skills?
options: A. The description rambles here and there.
B. There is too much emphasis on detail.
C. She is very good at controlling the pace of her description.
D. Her attempt at organising the material is not always successful.
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. Người bình luận nhận định: "Flanders shows herself adept at controlling the flow of information and keeping her story moving briskly forward (there is no dawdling...)". Điều này chứng minh tác giả kiểm soát rất xuất sắc tiến trình và nhịp độ của mạch kể chuyện. Bẫy: (A) sai vì văn phong đi nhanh và không lê thê, (B) sai vì chi tiết vật lý được ca ngợi là 'ấn tượng' chứ không phải quá đà, (D) sai vì sự sắp xếp được đánh giá là 'xuất sắc' ('excellent way of ordering material'). Chuyên đề: Reading — Detailed understanding.
questions.answerLocation: Flanders shows herself adept at controlling the flow of information and keeping her story moving briskly forward
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 5, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 33. Flanders’s description of the insects and pests in Victorian houses reveals that
options: A. houses with carpets attracted more of them.
B. people did not keep their kitchens clean.
C. people would go to hotels to escape from them.
D. clean bed linen without fleas was extremely rare.
questions.explanation: Đáp án D. Văn bản viết: "Bed linen was usually infested with fleas... 'possible to have too much Natural History in a bed'". Từ 'usually infested' (thường xuyên bị ký sinh) đồng nghĩa với việc ga trải giường sạch sẽ hoàn toàn không có bọ chét là cực kỳ hiếm hoi. Bẫy: (A) và (B) sai vì không có thông tin nói thảm hút côn trùng hay nhà bếp bẩn là nguyên nhân duy nhất, (C) sai vì ngay cả phòng khách sạn cao cấp của Beatrix Potter cũng tràn ngập bọ chét nên đi khách sạn không phải là để trốn chúng. Chuyên đề: Reading — Detailed understanding.
questions.answerLocation: Bed linen was usually infested with fleas
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 5, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 34. What is stated in the review about Flanders’s sources?
options: A. She passes off secondary sources as her own.
B. She does not claim to use original sources for her material.
C. She depends heavily on others’ views.
D. She uses new, previously undiscovered sources for her work.
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. Ở đoạn 4, người viết chỉ ra: "The Victorian House is heavily dependent on secondary sources, which means that all too often Flanders is quoting someone... who is referring to the original source". Điều này khẳng định tác giả phụ thuộc nặng nề vào các nguồn tài liệu thứ cấp và quan điểm của các nhà sử học khác. Bẫy: (A) sai vì cô ấy ghi rõ trích dẫn chứ không đạo văn, (B) sai vì cô ấy có tuyên bố tự tìm kiếm tài liệu gốc trong lời giới thiệu, (D) ngược lại hoàn toàn vì nguồn tài liệu này đã được giới sử học khai thác từ lâu. Chuyên đề: Reading — Detailed understanding.
questions.answerLocation: The Victorian House is heavily dependent on secondary sources
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 5, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 35. What is the ‘danger’ referred to by the reviewer when discussing Flanders’s reliance on processed research?
options: A. that the book might not contain original academic thought
B. that historical inaccuracies could be repeated
C. that Victorian life is compared too closely with the 1970s
D. that the lives of ordinary women are overlooked
questions.explanation: Đáp án A. Người bình luận cảnh báo: "...the danger is that Flanders is merely reproducing a paradigm of Victorian domestic life that was formed as far back as the mid 1970s". Mối nguy hiểm ở đây là cuốn sách chỉ lặp lại khuôn mẫu cũ của thế hệ nghiên cứu trước mà không mang lại khám phá hay tư duy học thuật độc lập, nguyên bản. Bẫy: (B), (C), (D) không phản ánh nội dung của mối nguy hiểm học thuật này. Chuyên đề: Reading — Detailed understanding.
questions.answerLocation: merely reproducing a paradigm of Victorian domestic life that was formed as far back as the mid 1970s
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 5, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 36. What criticism does the reviewer make about Flanders's narrative authority in the last paragraph?
options: A. She is not convincing as a genuine expert in her field.
B. What she has written is too commercially oriented.
C. She is not organised enough to be a good historical writer.
D. She attempts to mimic the tone of Victorian manual writers.
questions.explanation: Đáp án A. Người viết kết luận: "The problem, really, is Flanders’s voice, which implies an authority that simply is not backed up by her text". Điều này chỉ trích rằng giọng văn của cô ấy thể hiện sự uy quyền chuyên môn nhưng nội dung học thuật bên dưới lại không tương xứng, khiến cô ấy không thuyết phục như một chuyên gia thực thụ. Bẫy: (B) sai vì yếu tố thương mại được dùng để so sánh với Mrs. Beeton chứ không phải chỉ trích chính của cuốn sách, (C) sai vì tổ chức sách được khen ở phần đầu, (D) sai vì giọng văn của cô ấy giống như họ nhưng cô ấy không cố tình bắt chước họ làm mục tiêu viết. Chuyên đề: Reading — Evaluative understanding.
questions.answerLocation: implies an authority that simply is not backed up by her text.
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 5, CPE Practice Tests 3

sections.title: Part 6: Gapped Text
sections.instructions: You are going to read an extract from a story. Seven paragraphs have been removed from the extract. Choose from the paragraphs A-H the one which fits each gap (37-43). There is one extra paragraph which you do not need to use.
sections.passageHtml:
Part 6: Gapped Text

Tom Sponson, at fifty-three, was a thoroughly successful man. He had worked up a first-class business, married a charming wife, and built himself a good house in the London suburbs that was neither so modern as to be pretentious nor so conventional as to be dull. He had good taste and was always impeccably dressed. To any outsider, he did not seem to have any problems that might mar his happiness.

[ Gap 37 ]

All the same, for some time Tom had been aware that he was working very hard for very little. His wife, Louie, gave him a peck in the morning when he left for the office and a peck in the evening when he came home. He had never thought of complaining. His food was always there, beautifully prepared, and his clean clothes were neatly folded on his chair by the double bed.

[ Gap 38 ]

The children were even more preoccupied; the boy with his own work and friends, the girl with hers. They were polite to Tom, but if he came into the room when they were entertaining a friend, there was at once a feeling of constraint. A sudden hush fell, or the topic of conversation seemed to be changed hurriedly. He had noticed the same thing at work if he happened to go into his secretary's office; her telephone conversation would rapidly take on a more serious tone.

[ Gap 39 ]

In fact, if he asked what the joke was, his wife would say, ‘You wouldn’t understand’ or ‘I'll tell you afterwards,’ but she never did; she would put him off with some remark like, ‘Oh, it’s perfectly silly, about something April said’. He could hardly remember the day they had last sat down together to have a talk about his business. She had lately shown so little interest in his work that it occurred to him that he might as well not exist, were it not, of course, for the financial support he gave them.

[ Gap 40 ]

Immediately he felt that he could not stand any more of this existence; it was nonsense. It was not as though his wife and children depended any more on the business; he could sell it to a combine tomorrow, and it would support all of them in comfort. His wife could continue the same lifestyle as before with her social commitments and lavish budget, while their children would be independent.

[ Gap 41 ]

As he circled Trafalgar Square within a few hundred yards of his office, he told himself that he could not go on. There seemed no reason why he should subject himself to this pattern of events any longer. There were so many individuals like himself, heads down, eyes glazed, rushing to their work, caught in the same treadmill. But there were others too, chatting in cars or on the pavements, as if they would never tire of each other’s company. Were they the ones who had already had the courage to make the break? Instead of turning down the Strand out of the square, he drove straight on to a West End garage.

[ Gap 42 ]

It was February, but he was surprised, for a moment, to find that both of the hotels were closed. Only the village pub was still open for visitors, and as he sat in the coffee-room, he appeared to be the only visitor except for a commercial traveller. In the course of their conversation, Tom realized fully that he had been living a life of hypocrisy, boasting to his colleagues of the sympathetic devotion of his wife and children but, in fact, nothing more than a cipher in his home.

[ Gap 43 ]

In the hall of the private entrance, the clerk was waiting for him on his return with the register, and, upon an impulse that, for the moment, he did not understand, he hesitated and wrote down a false name and a false address. He was surprised at himself as he detested such trickery, but later, lying upstairs on his bed with a book, he realized how necessary it had been, how wisely he had followed his impulse.

questionGroups.instruction: Choose from the paragraphs A-H the one which fits each gap (37-43).

questions.explanation: Đáp án H. Đoạn này thảo luận về người vợ Louie và cuộc sống bận rộn với các mối quan hệ xã hội của bà ấy: 'But it was obvious that her life was completely filled with the children...'. Nó kết nối hoàn hảo với đoạn đầu mô tả cuộc sống tưởng chừng hạnh phúc của Tom ('married a charming wife...') và dẫn dắt sang đoạn sau bắt đầu bằng: 'All the same, for some time Tom had been aware...'. Chuyên đề: Reading — Cohesion & Coherence.
questions.answerLocation: married a charming wife

questions.explanation: Đáp án E. Đoạn E giới thiệu về hai đứa con của Tom: Bob (19 tuổi, học khảo cổ tại Oxford) và April (16 tuổi, học sinh ngoan ngoãn đáng yêu). Nó đứng ngay trước đoạn nói về sự xa cách của chúng: 'The children were even more preoccupied...'. Chuyên đề: Reading — Cohesion & Coherence.
questions.answerLocation: The children were even more preoccupied

questions.explanation: Đáp án A. Đoạn A mô tả phản ứng của các con khi Tom bước vào phòng: 'But at home, the children never seemed to do this with their mother. He would find them all three, for instance, laughing...'. Đoạn tiếp theo bắt đầu bằng 'In fact, if he asked what the joke was, his wife would say, ‘You wouldn’t understand’'. Điều này tạo ra dòng chảy thông tin cực kỳ mạch lạc về sự cô độc của Tom ngay trong căn nhà của mình. Chuyên đề: Reading — Cohesion & Coherence.
questions.answerLocation: In fact, if he asked what the joke was

questions.explanation: Đáp án F. Đoạn F mô tả sự bất mật dâng trào của Tom khi nhận ra mình là kẻ thừa thãi: 'He said to himself, ‘It isn’t only that they don’t need me...’' và hành động quyết định bỏ trốn bằng cách giả vờ quên chào tạm biệt buổi sáng. Đoạn tiếp theo phản ánh trực tiếp tâm trạng đó: 'Immediately he felt that he could not stand any more of this existence...'. Chuyên đề: Reading — Cohesion & Coherence.
questions.answerLocation: Immediately he felt that he could not stand

questions.explanation: Đáp án B. Đoạn B mô tả sự đấu tranh tư tưởng của Tom về việc từ bỏ công việc kinh doanh đã gắn bó lâu năm để đổi lấy sự tự do: 'Of course, he would miss the business; it was his chief interest...'. Đoạn tiếp theo kể về hành trình lái xe đi làm nhưng lại suy nghĩ mông lung: 'As he circled Trafalgar Square...'. Chuyên đề: Reading — Cohesion & Coherence.
questions.answerLocation: As he circled Trafalgar Square within

questions.explanation: Đáp án G. Đoạn G ghi lại hành động nhảy lên chuyến tàu đi Westford của Tom cùng chiếc vali mới mua chứa đầy đồ dùng cá nhân: 'An hour later, he was on the train for Westford...'. Đoạn tiếp theo bắt đầu bằng: 'It was February, but he was surprised... only the village pub was still open...'. Chuyên đề: Reading — Cohesion & Coherence.
questions.answerLocation: It was February, but he was surprised

questions.explanation: Đáp án D. Đoạn D mô tả hành động đi bộ dọc bờ biển trong cơn mưa tầm tã và quay trở về quán rượu với ý định đọc tiểu thuyết trinh thám: '...He turned back to the pub, intending to read one of his new crime novels.' Nó kết nối xuất sắc với đoạn tiếp theo mô tả cảnh anh ta bước vào đại sảnh: 'In the hall of the private entrance, the clerk was waiting for him on his return...'. Chuyên đề: Reading — Cohesion & Coherence.
questions.answerLocation: In the hall of the private entrance

paragraphOptions.label: A
paragraphOptions.text: But at home, the children never seemed to do this with their mother. He would find them all three, for instance, laughing at something and when he came in they would stop and gaze at him as if he had shot up through the floor. He would feel most uncomfortable, as if he were intruding on the most private of matters.

paragraphOptions.label: B
paragraphOptions.text: Of course, he would miss the business; it was his chief interest. He had started it in his thirties and worked it up from nothing. But if he had to give it up for the sake of freedom, a break in this senseless life, he could do even that. Yes, joyfully.

paragraphOptions.label: C
paragraphOptions.text: All at once he started back. He had actually been on the brink of running away, declaring himself a fugitive. And from what? Nonsense? To run away from nonsense, a made-up delusion, that would be madness. ‘Words,’ he said. ‘It’s all words. They live on words; they can’t understand anything but words. They think words. You talk to them, but you might as well be babbling in a madhouse to a lot of tape recorders; they only get a lot of noises.’

paragraphOptions.label: D
paragraphOptions.text: It was as though that moment when he dodged the customary good-bye had broken a contact. The conveyor belt on which his life had been caught up had stopped as if at a short circuit. Wasn't it simply by a stroke of luck that he had broken out into the clean air? And as if the words themselves demanded the action, he put on his mackintosh and went out. Outside he felt his strength. He had made a big decision and saved his life. He went down to the beach and walked close to the waves until the rain grew heavy. He turned back to the pub, intending to read one of his new crime novels.

paragraphOptions.label: E
paragraphOptions.text: His son, Bob, nineteen, was doing well at Oxford; he was studying Archaeology, not having the practical business acumen of his father. His daughter, April, aged sixteen, was at a good school and had no wish to use make-up, wear short frocks, or flirt. She still regarded herself too young for these trifling amusements. Yet, she was lively, affectionate, and thoroughly enjoyed life.

paragraphOptions.label: F
paragraphOptions.text: He said to himself, ‘It isn’t only that they don’t need me, but I’m a nuisance to them. I’m in the way. I’m superfluous.’ One morning when he was about to get into his car and his wife had come out to say good-bye, he suddenly made an excuse, saying, ‘Just a moment, I’ve left behind a letter,’ and went back indoors. Then he dashed out to the car and drove off, pretending to forget that good-bye had not been said.

paragraphOptions.label: G
paragraphOptions.text: An hour later, he was on the train for Westford, a seaside place where he had once spent a summer holiday before his marriage, with three friends from college. On the luggage rack was a new suitcase containing new pyjamas, shoes, as for a holiday by the sea, and even new paperbacks for a wet day.

paragraphOptions.label: H
paragraphOptions.text: But it was obvious that her life was completely filled with the children, her clothes, charities, bridge, tennis, her friends, and parties. She made no demands on the few hours of leisure time he spent at home but he often wondered if it mattered to her where he was.

sections.title: Part 7: Multiple Matching
sections.instructions: You are going to read an article about deep-sea bioluminescence. For questions 44-53, choose from the scientists (A-E). The scientists may be chosen more than once.
sections.passageHtml:
Oceanic Illumination

Deep in the ocean's twilight zone, thousands of meters below the surface where sunlight never penetrates, a silent revolution of living light takes place. Five oceanographers share their insights into deep-sea bioluminescence and its evolutionary implications.

A. Dr. Alistair Finch — The Chemical Wizards of the Abyss

To produce light in the pitch-black abyss requires a sophisticated biochemical synthesis. Bioluminescence is primarily driven by the reaction between luciferin and its catalytic enzyme, luciferase. However, synthesizing luciferin demands a high metabolic cost, which is why organisms do not flash continuously. Evolutionary critics once dismissed bioluminescence as a mere metabolic byproduct or an evolutionary accident of cell chemistry, but modern deep-sea research has thoroughly debunked this skepticism. The chemical pathways have been refined over millions of years, showing that every flash is a highly optimized energetic expenditure designed for survival in a resource-scarce environment.

B. Dr. Sylvia Earle-Mendez — Stealth and Counter-Illumination

In the open midwater, there is nowhere to hide. Predators looking upwards scan the faint silhouette of prey cast against the dim sunlight downwelling from above. To combat this, many fish have developed the exquisite strategy of counter-illumination. By emitting light from specialized photophores on their underbellies, they match the color and intensity of the downwelling sunlight, effectively erasing their silhouette. This masking mechanism is so dynamic and precise that certain species of squid and fish can instantly adjust their luminescent output to match rapid changes in cloud cover or shifting surface ripples, remaining completely invisible to hungry eyes below.

C. Prof. Kenji Takahashi — Lures, Traps, and Symbiotic Alliances

Rather than hiding, some deep-sea residents use bioluminescence as a lethal attraction. The most famous example is the anglerfish, which dangles a glowing bulb at the end of a modified dorsal spine. What makes this trap truly fascinating is that the fish itself does not produce the light; instead, it relies on a symbiotic relationship with bioluminescent bacteria housed inside the organ. The bacteria receive a secure, nutrient-rich environment, while the host gains a powerful lure to capture prey. This symbiotic alliance represents one of the most sophisticated examples of co-dependent evolutionary history in the marine world, where two entirely different domains of life have merged their survival strategies.

D. Dr. Elena Petrova — Alarm Systems and the Burglar Alarm Effect

Defense mechanisms in the deep sea are incredibly creative. When attacked, certain species of copepods and jellyfish do not merely flash to startle the attacker. Instead, they activate a 'burglar alarm' strategy, releasing a brilliant, long-lasting burst of light. The primary goal is not to defend themselves directly, but to illuminate their attacker, making the predator highly visible to even larger, top-tier predators that might be cruising nearby. In some cases, jellies release sticky, glowing bioluminescent secretions that adhere to their attackers, marking them with a bright beacon that guarantees they will soon be hunted down by other creatures, effectively turning the predator into prey.

E. Dr. Marcus Thorne — Visual Communication Networks

Beyond predator-prey dynamics, bioluminescence serves as a rich language. In the darkness, finding a mate is a monumental challenge. Species-specific flashing patterns allow organisms to identify potential mates and signal reproductive readiness. Interestingly, the visual systems of these deep-sea creatures have evolved in perfect tandem with their light-emitting capabilities. Their highly specialized photoreceptors are specifically tuned to the exact blue-green wavelength of bioluminescent light, which propagates furthest through seawater. This co-evolution of visual organs and luminescent displays suggests a complex, interconnected communication network that spans the vast, dark plains of our oceans.

questionGroups.instruction: For questions 44-53, choose the correct scientist (A-E) using the separate answer sheet.

questions.instruction: 44. Mentions how some organisms utilize a chemical decoy that adheres to their attackers.
questions.explanation: Đáp án D. Tiến sĩ Elena Petrova đề cập đến việc sứa giải phóng chất tiết phát quang bám dính vào kẻ tấn công ('bioluminescent secretions... that adhere to their attackers'), biến kẻ săn mồi thành ngọn hải đăng di động. Chuyên đề: Reading — Multiple Matching.
questions.answerLocation: jellies release sticky, glowing bioluminescent secretions that adhere to their attackers
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 45. Asserts that the visual systems of deep-sea creatures evolved in tandem with their light-emitting capabilities.
questions.explanation: Đáp án E. Tiến sĩ Marcus Thorne lập luận rằng cơ quan thị giác của các sinh vật biển sâu đã tiến hóa song song đồng thời với khả năng phát sáng của chúng ('visual systems... evolved in perfect tandem with their light-emitting capabilities'). Chuyên đề: Reading — Multiple Matching.
questions.answerLocation: visual systems of these deep-sea creatures have evolved in perfect tandem with their light-emitting capabilities.
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 46. Explains a defensive strategy that relies on simulating the light profile of the environment above.
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. Tiến sĩ Sylvia Earle-Mendez giải thích cơ chế ngụy trang bằng cách phát ánh sáng từ bụng để khớp với ánh sáng từ mặt trời chiếu xuống ('emitting light... to match the downwelling sunlight'). Chuyên đề: Reading — Multiple Matching.
questions.answerLocation: emitting light from specialized photophores on their underbellies, they match the color and intensity of the downwelling sunlight
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 47. Disproves the notion that light production in marine organisms was merely a random mutation without functional purpose.
questions.explanation: Đáp án A. Tiến sĩ Alistair Finch bác bỏ quan điểm cho rằng phát quang sinh học chỉ là một tai nạn tiến hóa ngẫu nhiên không có mục đích ('dismissed bioluminescence as a mere metabolic byproduct... modern research has thoroughly debunked this skepticism'). Chuyên đề: Reading — Multiple Matching.
questions.answerLocation: dismissed bioluminescence as a mere metabolic byproduct or an evolutionary accident... modern deep-sea research has thoroughly debunked this skepticism.
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 48. Identifies a predatory method that employs live bacteria to attract food.
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. Giáo sư Kenji Takahashi chỉ ra loài cá rồng/cá anglerfish sử dụng mối quan hệ cộng sinh với vi khuẩn phát sáng sống trong cơ quan của mình để dụ mồi ('relies on a symbiotic relationship with bioluminescent bacteria... to capture prey'). Chuyên đề: Reading — Multiple Matching.
questions.answerLocation: relies on a symbiotic relationship with bioluminescent bacteria housed inside the organ.
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 49. Highlights the remarkable ability of certain organisms to dynamically regulate their light intensity based on atmospheric shifts.
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. Tiến sĩ Sylvia Earle-Mendez nhấn mạnh khả năng tự điều chỉnh cường độ phát sáng dựa trên sự thay đổi nhanh chóng của các hiện tượng thời tiết/mây che phủ ở tầng mặt ('adjust their luminescent output to match rapid changes in cloud cover'). Chuyên đề: Reading — Multiple Matching.
questions.answerLocation: instantly adjust their luminescent output to match rapid changes in cloud cover
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 50. Describes how an organism uses light to watchdog and attract a larger threat to its immediate attacker.
questions.explanation: Đáp án D. Tiến sĩ Elena Petrova giải thích chiến lược 'burglar alarm' (chuông báo trộm), chiếu sáng kẻ tấn công để thu hút các kẻ săn mồi siêu cấp lớn hơn đến tiêu diệt kẻ tấn công đó ('illuminate their attacker, making the predator highly visible to even larger, top-tier predators'). Chuyên đề: Reading — Multiple Matching.
questions.answerLocation: illuminate their attacker, making the predator highly visible to even larger, top-tier predators
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 51. Points out that the blue-green light spectrum is optimal for transmission through seawater.
questions.explanation: Đáp án E. Tiến sĩ Marcus Thorne đề cập việc ánh sáng phát quang màu xanh lam-xanh lá cây truyền đi được xa nhất dưới nước ('exact blue-green wavelength... which propagates furthest through seawater'). Chuyên đề: Reading — Multiple Matching.
questions.answerLocation: exact blue-green wavelength of bioluminescent light, which propagates furthest through seawater.
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 52. Mentions the high energy cost associated with the biochemical synthesis of luciferin.
questions.explanation: Đáp án A. Tiến sĩ Alistair Finch nhấn mạnh rằng việc tổng hợp hợp chất luciferin tiêu tốn rất nhiều năng lượng trao đổi chất của sinh vật ('synthesizing luciferin demands a high metabolic cost'). Chuyên đề: Reading — Multiple Matching.
questions.answerLocation: synthesizing luciferin demands a high metabolic cost
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 53. Describes a relationship between two species that represents a pinnacle of co-dependent evolutionary history.
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. Giáo sư Kenji Takahashi mô tả mối quan hệ cộng sinh giữa cá và vi khuẩn là ví dụ tinh tế nhất của lịch sử tiến hóa đồng phụ thuộc ('symbiotic alliance represents one of the most sophisticated examples of co-dependent evolutionary history'). Chuyên đề: Reading — Multiple Matching.
questions.answerLocation: symbiotic alliance represents one of the most sophisticated examples of co-dependent evolutionary history
questions.source: Practice Test 5, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3
